examples: Most of the spectacular 'Catts' seen in corsages are hybrids of such tropical American species as these Cattleya trianaei.
Formation and morphogenesis of the protocorms of Cattleya hybrid were investigated in cytology by light micros copy and electronic microscopy.
Mexican epiphytic orchid with glaucous gray-green leaves and lemon - to golden-yellow flowers appearing only partially opened; sometimes placed in genus Cattleya.
